feature or request"},{"id":4174041340,"node_id":"LA_kwDODP2Uvs74ytD8","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/labels/discussion","name":"discussion","color":"0052CC","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2022-08-08T11:37:04Z","updated_at":"2024-06-27T15:12:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Currently `from_mcmcchains` assumes that all variable names are single-bracket-delimited or dot-delimited. However, quite complicated names are possible:

```julia
julia> using Turing, LinearAlgebra

julia> @model function foo()
           a ~ Normal()
           bar = (b=Matrix{typeof(a)}(undef, 2, 3), c=Vector{typeof(a)}(undef, 3))
           bar.b[1, :] .~ Normal(a)
           bar.b[2, 1:2] ~ MvNormal(I(2))
           bar.b[2, 3:end] .~ Normal(a)
           bar.c[end:-1:1] .~ Normal(a)
       end
foo (generic function with 2 methods)

julia> chns = sample(foo(), NUTS(), 1_000)
┌ Info: Found initial step size
└   ϵ = 1.6
Sampling 100%|█████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████| Time: 0:00:01
Chains MCMC chain (1000×22×1 Array{Float64, 3}):

Iterations        = 501:1:1500
Number of chains  = 1
Samples per chain = 1000
Wall duration     = 1.21 seconds
Compute duration  = 1.21 seconds
parameters        = a, bar.b[1,:][1], bar.b[1,:][2], bar.b[1,:][3], bar.b[2,1:2][1], bar.b[2,1:2][2], bar.b[2,3:3][1], bar.c[3:-1:1][1], bar.c[3:-1:1][2], bar.c[3:-1:1][3]
internals         = lp, n_steps, is_accept, acceptance_rate, log_density, hamiltonian_energy, hamiltonian_energy_error, max_hamiltonian_energy_error, tree_depth, numerical_error, step_size, nom_step_size

Summary Statistics
        parameters      mean       std   naive_se      mcse         ess      rhat   ess_per_sec 
            Symbol   Float64   Float64    Float64   Float64     Float64   Float64       Float64 

                 a    0.0278    0.9966     0.0315    0.0724    139.5528    1.0152      115.1426
     bar.b[1,:][1]    0.0178    1.4143     0.0447    0.0841    205.0380    1.0096      169.1733
     bar.b[1,:][2]    0.0268    1.3678     0.0433    0.0765    292.3916    1.0072      241.2472
     bar.b[1,:][3]    0.0395    1.3866     0.0438    0.0852    234.2846    1.0061      193.3041
   bar.b[2,1:2][1]    0.0395    1.0091     0.0319    0.0260   1017.5267    0.9990      839.5435
   bar.b[2,1:2][2]   -0.0270    0.9839     0.0311    0.0260   1495.1411    0.9990     1233.6147
   bar.b[2,3:3][1]    0.0506    1.3798     0.0436    0.0724    347.4877    1.0032      286.7061
  bar.c[3:-1:1][1]    0.0156    1.4357     0.0454    0.0835    275.8889    1.0079      227.6311
  bar.c[3:-1:1][2]   -0.0010    1.4186     0.0449    0.0720    362.6178    1.0072      299.1896
  bar.c[3:-1:1][3]    0.0146    1.4061     0.0445    0.0783    333.0312    1.0069      274.7783

Quantiles
        parameters      2.5%     25.0%     50.0%     75.0%     97.5% 
            Symbol   Float64   Float64   Float64   Float64   Float64 

                 a   -1.8465   -0.6502   -0.0164    0.7037    2.0664
     bar.b[1,:][1]   -2.7699   -0.9711    0.0028    0.9771    2.7813
     bar.b[1,:][2]   -2.5305   -0.9451    0.0408    0.9232    2.7366
     bar.b[1,:][3]   -2.6779   -0.9212    0.0311    1.0146    2.7213
   bar.b[2,1:2][1]   -1.8999   -0.6486    0.0397    0.7054    2.1453
   bar.b[2,1:2][2]   -1.9572   -0.7017   -0.0031    0.6454    1.8877
   bar.b[2,3:3][1]   -2.6416   -0.8997   -0.0014    0.9453    3.0213
  bar.c[3:-1:1][1]   -2.8470   -0.9299   -0.0128    0.9509    2.8362
  bar.c[3:-1:1][2]   -2.8268   -0.9619    0.0671    0.9812    2.7441
  bar.c[3:-1:1][3]   -2.7313   -0.9267    0.0001    0.9474    2.8913
```

If we call `from_mcmcchains` on this, we get an uninformative error.

Ideally we would like to get an `InferenceData` with `bar.b` and `bar.c` as variables. However, since the modeler can arbitrarily index and reindex and call `getproperty` to make arbitrarily complicated types, always doing the right thing is probably not possible. Also, MCMCChains's own machinery for combining flattened parameters into parameter arrays doesn't do a great job here.

For the short term then, I think it makes the most sense to raise an informative error of splitting by brackets produces anything more complicated than a tuple of integer indices. If users find this constraining and open issues, we can discuss supporting slightly more complicated indexing syntaxes.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/comments/2194981951","html_url":"https://github.com/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211#issuecomment-2194981951","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211","id":2194981951,"node_id":"IC_kwDODP2Uvs6C1MQ_","user":{"login":"SamuelBrand1","id":48288458,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ4Mjg4NDU4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/48288458?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1","html_url":"https://github.com/SamuelBrand1","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-27T15:12:25Z","updated_at":"2024-06-27T15:12:25Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Was there ever a resolution on this? I think I'm hitting an issue with a project I'm working on.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/comments/2194981951/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

If we call `from_mcmcchains` on this, we get an uninformative error.

Ideally we would like to get an `InferenceData` with `bar.b` and `bar.c` as variables. However, since the modeler can arbitrarily index and reindex and call `getproperty` to make arbitrarily complicated types, always doing the right thing is probably not possible. Also, MCMCChains's own machinery for combining flattened parameters into parameter arrays doesn't do a great job here.

For the short term then, I think it makes the most sense to raise an informative error of splitting by brackets produces anything more complicated than a tuple of integer indices. If users find this constraining and open issues, we can discuss supporting slightly more complicated indexing syntaxes.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/comments/2195280386","html_url":"https://github.com/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211#issuecomment-2195280386","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/arviz-devs/ArviZ.jl/issues/211","id":2195280386,"node_id":"IC_kwDODP2Uvs6C2VIC","user":{"login":"SamuelBrand1","id":48288458,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ4Mjg4NDU4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/48288458?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1","html_url":"https://github.com/SamuelBrand1","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SamuelBrand1/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-27T17:31:06Z","updated_at":"2024-06-27T17:31:06Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> Okay, I think we can support this by removing support for the old `.` syntax for separating indices. If we call `from_mcmcchains` on this, we get an uninformative error.

Ideally we would like to get an `InferenceData` with `bar.b` and `bar.c` as variables. However, since the modeler can arbitrarily index and reindex and call `getproperty` to make arbitrarily complicated types, always doing the right thing is probably not possible. Also, MCMCChains's own machinery for combining flattened parameters into parameter arrays doesn't do a great job here.

For the short term then, I think it makes the most sense to raise an informative error of splitting by brackets produces anything more complicated than a tuple of integer indices.
